title: pi tool surface + how pi-bound bounds/excludes it
question: What is the full tool surface a pi (sub)agent sees, and by what mechanism does `pi-context pi-bound` narrow/exclude that surface?
method: code-inspection of pi-bound.ts + cli.ts + ops-registry.ts + auth-gate.ts + the four extensions' tool registrations + pi-bound.test.ts; cross-referenced against the generated SKILL.md tool tags and CLAUDE.md's `pi -p` usage.
findings_summary: pi-bound is a pi-context-cli process mode (packages/pi-context-cli/src/pi-bound.ts), not a substrate op: cli.ts:963-965 dispatches the bare `pi-bound` verb before resolveOp. It bounds the tool surface by default-deny via an explicit `--tools` allowlist handed to a real pi child process (`pi --tools <csv> ...passthrough`, pi-bound.ts:305) — an in-process TS port of scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh. The allowlist (composePiBoundTools, pi-bound.ts:161-170) is the deduped union of three sets: (1) staticTools — every `<tool name>` parsed from the two skill packages' SKILL.md (the extension surface: pi-context's 57 surface:use ops + pi-workflows 9 + pi-agent-dispatch 6 + pi-behavior-monitors 5); (2) BUILTIN_READONLY_TOOLS = read,ls,grep,find (unconditional, pi-bound.ts:32); (3) the active substrate's config.tool_operations[] composites. `--grant <id>` (repeatable) is the only pi-bound-specific lever and narrows ONLY the composite portion (grants.length>0 ? grants : declaredComposites); static + read-only are unconditional. Everything else (--model/--no-skills/-c/--mode) is pi passthrough (pi-bound.ts:76-77, forwarded at :305). Exclusion is by omission: a tool not in a SKILL.md, not a read-only built-in, and not a declared composite is absent — so pi's mutating built-ins write/edit/bash are excluded by default. A distinct second exclusion layer is the pi-agent-dispatch auth-gate (auth-gate.ts:148-161): AUTH_REQUIRED_TOOLS (the per-package authGated/gatedTools union) is blocked at call time when ctx.hasUI===false, so Bucket-2 tools are effectively excluded in a non-interactive session even when allowlisted. pi-bound does NOT reuse the pi-jit-agents executeAgent grant-clamp (child subset-of parent, jit-runtime.ts:483-491) — that is an independent in-process layer. The pi -p --tools path (CLAUDE.md CLI-Access) bounds via the SAME pi --tools primitive with a caller-authored list, distinct from pi-bound's composed list. The complete pi built-in tool registry and pi's --tools match semantics are pi-runtime-internal (not in this repo); the live set is enumerable via the surface:process list-tools op (getAllTools/getActiveTools). Verify exclusion: call list-tools inside the session, or inspect the composed --tools CSV via the exported composePiBoundTools/deriveSkillToolNames.
stale_conditions: composePiBoundTools (pi-bound.ts:161-170) changes its union sources (the SKILL-derived static set, the read-only built-ins, or the composite source)
the pi-agent-dispatch auth-gate AUTH_REQUIRED_TOOLS derivation (auth-gate.ts) changes

citations.label: allowlist composition — union of static SKILL tools, read-only built-ins, composites
bare pi-bound verb dispatch before resolveOp
execution-time auth-gate — blocks gated tools when ctx.hasUI=false
list-tools surface:process introspection op (live tool enumeration)
